questions.question: What is the primary goal of Writing Clean Code?
options: B) To enhance software quality and maintainability
questions.explanation: Writing Clean Code aims to make the code more readable, maintainable, and efficient, thus enhancing software quality.

questions.question: Which technique is used to identify and prioritize software defects?
questions.explanation: Test-Driven Development (TDD) is a technique used to write tests before writing the code, thus identifying and prioritizing software defects.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Code Reviews?
options: B) To enhance software quality and catch bugs
questions.explanation: Code Reviews are used to evaluate and improve the quality of code, catch bugs, and ensure that the code adheres to the coding standards.

questions.question: Which technique is used to identify and address potential problems in software development?
questions.explanation: Design Patterns are reusable solutions to common problems in software development, thus identifying and addressing potential problems.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of Test-Driven Development (TDD)?
options: B) To enhance software quality and maintainability
questions.explanation: Test-Driven Development (TDD) aims to write high-quality code by writing tests before writing the code, thus enhancing software quality and maintainability.
